introduction: Write the Introduction section (3-4 paragraphs). Motivate the problem, state the specific hypothesis, summarise contributions at the end of the section using an \begin{itemize}...\end{itemize} environment.
related_work: Write the Related Work section (2-3 paragraphs) situating this work among prior research. Cite at least one of the supplied keys.
method: Write the Method section describing the approach with enough precision that a reader could reimplement it. Use \begin{equation}...\end{equation} when equations add clarity.
experiments: Write the Experiments section describing setup, baselines, metrics, and exactly what was run. Be specific about hyperparameters, datasets, and compute. Keep to 2-3 paragraphs unless results are supplied.
results: Write the Results section. ONLY describe results supported by the supplied experiment log. Use \begin{table}...\end{table} for tabular data. If no results are supplied, write: 'Results forthcoming.'
discussion: Write the Discussion section (2 paragraphs). Interpret results, acknowledge limitations, and compare to the hypothesis.
conclusion: Write the Conclusion section (1 short paragraph). Restate the contribution and outline 1-2 directions for future work.
